strengths: Genuine distributed-systems depth: operated microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby with low-latency, async I/O patterns directly relevant to real-time inference pipelines.
Early and current hands-on exposure to agentic infrastructure: contributing to MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq, showing practical engagement with the AI-agent paradigm.
Polyglot, full-stack craftsman with consistent event-driven and TDD discipline across 17 years and multiple domains — a strong engineering foundation for building reliable systems.
concerns: No engineering leadership track record: the entire career is individual-contributor and staff-level; there is no evidence of building, scaling, or managing an engineering organization of any size.

AI-agent work is tooling and framework support, not production multi-agent systems owning financial decisions — the role requires demonstrated production operation of stateful agents with real commercial consequences.

No meaningful fintech-infrastructure experience: RIDE Capital involvement was general software craftsmanship on an investment platform, not credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk modeling, or regulatory compliance.
response.justification: Bogdan is a highly skilled, experienced individual contributor with genuine backend depth and early-stage exposure to agentic tooling, but the CTO role is fundamentally an executive engineering-leadership position requiring demonstrated team-scaling, organizational design, and production ownership of high-stakes AI systems in regulated fintech contexts — none of which appear in the resume. The gap between his current staff/senior-developer trajectory and the seniority, leadership scope, and domain-specific requirements of this role is substantial.
